steps.title: Join contrasting clauses
steps.content: The text has two independent clauses: Leibniz wheel calculators were popular, and they were eventually replaced. The relationship is contrast, so the sentence needs a coordinating conjunction with the proper punctuation.

steps.title: Avoid run-ons and fragments
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice, and Choice B creates a fused sentence. Choice D uses that where it cannot join the two clauses grammatically. The answer is C.
